channel.title: Florida Impact Windows & Doors Blog
channel.description: Expert advice on impact windows, hurricane protection, and home improvement for South Florida homeowners.

item.title: Why Impact Doors Are Just as Important as Impact Windows in South Florida
item.description: Many homeowners focus on windows and overlook their doors. We explain why every entry point in your South Florida home needs impact-rated protection.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows for Historic Homes in South Florida
item.description: Installing impact windows in a historic South Florida home requires balancing preservation with protection. Learn about the options and approval processes for historic properties.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Common Impact Window Problems and How to Fix Them
item.description: Even the best impact windows can develop issues over time. We cover the most common problems South Florida homeowners encounter and the proper solutions for each.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: The Environmental Benefits of Impact Windows in South Florida
item.description: Impact windows do more than protect your home. They reduce energy consumption, lower carbon emissions, and contribute to a more sustainable South Florida. Here is how.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows and FEMA Flood Zone Requirements in South Florida
item.description: If your South Florida home is in a FEMA flood zone, your window installation may need to meet additional requirements. We explain how flood zones affect impact window specifications.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Coastal vs Inland: Different Impact Window Needs Across South Florida
item.description: Your location within South Florida significantly affects your impact window requirements. Learn how coastal and inland locations differ in wind exposure, salt air, and code requirements.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: How Impact Windows Reduce Condensation in South Florida Homes
item.description: Window condensation is a common problem in South Florida's humid climate. Discover how modern impact windows help minimize condensation and the moisture problems it causes.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Second-Story Impact Window Installation: Unique Challenges and Solutions
item.description: Installing impact windows on upper floors requires special equipment and techniques. Learn about the unique challenges of second-story and multi-story impact window installations.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Hurricane Season Preparation: A Month-by-Month Guide for South Florida Homeowners
item.description: Do not wait until a storm is approaching to prepare. Follow our month-by-month guide to get your South Florida home ready for hurricane season well in advance.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows for Bathrooms: Privacy and Protection Combined
item.description: Bathroom impact windows need to balance privacy with natural light and hurricane protection. Here are the best options for bathroom windows in South Florida homes.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Window Warranties: What Every South Florida Homeowner Should Know
item.description: Not all impact window warranties are created equal. We break down what to look for in manufacturer and contractor warranties to protect your investment.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Multi-Point Locking Systems on Impact Windows and Doors Explained
item.description: Multi-point locks are a critical feature of impact windows and doors. We explain how they work and why they matter for both hurricane protection and home security in South Florida.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: The History of Impact Windows in South Florida: From Hurricane Andrew to Today
item.description: Impact windows became a requirement after Hurricane Andrew devastated South Florida in 1992. Learn how this industry evolved from that pivotal moment to the advanced products available today.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows and Smart Home Integration in South Florida
item.description: Modern impact windows can work with smart home systems for automated ventilation, sensors, and security. Discover how to integrate your impact windows with smart technology.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: How to Choose a Reliable Impact Window Contractor in South Florida
item.description: Hiring the wrong contractor can lead to costly problems. We share the key factors to look for when selecting an impact window installer in South Florida.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows for Commercial Properties in South Florida
item.description: Commercial buildings in South Florida have different impact window requirements than residential homes. Learn about the unique considerations for storefronts, offices, and commercial buildings.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Best Impact Windows for Waterfront Homes in South Florida
item.description: Waterfront properties face unique challenges from salt air, higher wind exposures, and flooding. Here is what we recommend for impact windows on South Florida waterfront homes.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Window Glass Thickness Options: What You Need to Know
item.description: Not all impact glass is the same thickness. We explain the different glass configurations available and help you understand which thickness is right for your South Florida home.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: How Impact Windows Protect Against Flying Debris During Hurricanes
item.description: Flying debris is the number one cause of window failure during hurricanes. Learn exactly how impact windows are engineered to withstand projectile impacts and keep your South Florida home sealed.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Custom Impact Windows: Creating Unique Designs for South Florida Homes
item.description: Not every window opening is standard, and we love the custom projects. Here is what you need to know about custom impact windows for unique shapes and oversized openings.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Maintaining Impact Window Tracks and Hardware in South Florida's Climate
item.description: Sliding windows that stick or jam are one of the most common service calls we get. Most of the time, it is a simple maintenance issue. Here is how to keep yours running smoothly.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Laminated Glass vs Tempered Glass: Why Laminated Wins for Hurricane Protection
item.description: We only install laminated glass in our impact windows, and there is a very good reason for that. Here is why it is the only glass type that truly protects your home in a hurricane.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Window Labeling: How to Read the Sticker on Your Window
item.description: We teach every customer how to read their window labels because those stickers contain everything you need to verify your windows are code-compliant. Here is what to look for.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Condo and HOA Impact Window Installation in South Florida
item.description: We do a lot of condo and HOA work, and the process is different from a single-family home. Here is what we have learned about navigating association rules and shared responsibilities.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Are Impact Windows Tax Deductible in Florida?
item.description: We get asked about tax benefits for impact windows all the time. Here is what we have learned from 15 years of helping South Florida homeowners navigate the financial side of their projects.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: When to Replace vs Repair Your Impact Windows in South Florida
item.description: We get called out to inspect older impact windows regularly, and we can usually tell right away whether they need repair or replacement. Here are the signs we look for.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows and Home Value: What South Florida Buyers Want
item.description: In our 15 years in the business, we have seen firsthand how impact windows boost South Florida home values and make properties sell faster. Here is what we know from real transactions.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows and Home Security: Dual Protection for South Florida
item.description: Storm protection is what sells impact windows, but the security benefit is what our customers rave about most. We have seen these windows stop break-in attempts in their tracks.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Vinyl vs Aluminum Impact Window Frames: The Best Choice for South Florida
item.description: We work with both vinyl and aluminum frames every day, and each has its strengths. Here is our real-world comparison based on thousands of installations in South Florida.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Lessons from Past Hurricanes: Why Impact Windows Matter in South Florida
item.description: We have been in this industry long enough to remember the aftermath of Andrew, Wilma, and Irma. Each storm taught South Florida hard lessons about window protection, and we have applied every one of them.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Noise Reduction with Impact Windows: Enjoying Peace and Quiet in South Florida
item.description: Some of the best feedback we get from customers is about how quiet their homes are after we install impact windows. If you live near a busy road or airport, the difference is night and day.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Preparing Your Home for Impact Window Installation Day
item.description: After 15 years of installations, we know exactly what homeowners should do before our crew arrives. Here is our tried-and-true preparation checklist.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Matching Impact Windows to Your South Florida Home's Architectural Style
item.description: We have matched impact windows to every architectural style you can find in South Florida. Here is our guide to choosing the right windows for your home's design.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Flood and Wind Protection: How Impact Windows Handle Both Threats
item.description: In our years on job sites after storms, we have seen firsthand how wind and water work together to destroy homes. Here is how impact windows defend against both threats.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Low-E Glass and Energy-Efficient Impact Windows in South Florida
item.description: We always recommend Low-E glass to our customers because we have seen what it does to their electric bills. Here is how this technology works and why it matters in South Florida.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Window Requirements for Older South Florida Homes
item.description: We work on a lot of older South Florida homes, and homeowners always ask if they are required to upgrade their windows. Here is the straight answer based on current code.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: UV Protection and Furniture Savings with Impact Windows
item.description: We have walked into homes where the furniture looked brand new years after our installation, all because impact windows block nearly all UV rays. Here is how that works.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Creating a Family Hurricane Plan: The Role of Impact Windows
item.description: Impact windows take a huge weight off your shoulders during a hurricane, but they are just one part of a solid family preparedness plan. Here is what we tell every customer about the bigger picture.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Entry Doors: Protecting Your South Florida Home's Front Door
item.description: Your front door is one of the biggest weak points we see on homes without impact protection. Here is what we recommend based on thousands of entry door installations.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Dealing with Salt Air Corrosion on Impact Windows in Coastal South Florida
item.description: We install a lot of windows in coastal homes, and salt air is no joke. Here is what we recommend to keep your frames and hardware protected from corrosion.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Understanding Hurricane Categories and What They Mean for Your Windows
item.description: We have seen what Category 3 and above storms do to unprotected windows. Here is what we know from real-world experience about how each hurricane category impacts your home.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Sliding Glass Doors: The Complete Buyer's Guide for South Florida
item.description: We install more impact sliding glass doors than almost any other product. They are the centerpiece of South Florida living, and choosing the right one makes all the difference.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Understanding the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone in Miami-Dade and Broward
item.description: If you live in Miami-Dade or Broward County, you are in the HVHZ, the toughest building zone in the country. We know these requirements inside and out. Here is what they mean for you.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: PGT vs CGI Impact Windows: Comparing Top Brands for South Florida
item.description: We carry both PGT and CGI and have installed thousands of windows from each. Here is our hands-on comparison to help you choose the right brand for your project.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Storm-Proofing Your South Florida Home: A Complete Guide
item.description: We storm-proof homes for a living, and we can tell you that impact windows are just one piece of the puzzle. Here is the full checklist we use for every property we protect.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Wind Mitigation Inspections: Maximizing Your Insurance Discount
item.description: After every installation, we tell our customers to schedule a wind mitigation inspection immediately. It is the fastest way to start saving on your insurance. Here is how to prepare.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Understanding Impact Window Ratings and Certifications
item.description: We break down the certifications and ratings that actually matter when buying impact windows in South Florida, based on what we deal with on every project.

item.title: DIY vs Professional Impact Window Installation: Why You Need a Licensed Contractor

item.title: Choosing the Right Frame Color for Your South Florida Impact Windows
item.description: Frame color is one of the most fun decisions our customers get to make. Here are the colors we are installing most in South Florida right now and our tips for choosing.

item.description: We created this seasonal maintenance schedule based on what we see during service calls. Follow it and your impact windows will last decades without issues.

item.title: How to Prepare Your Windows for Hurricane Season in South Florida
item.description: After 15 years installing impact windows in South Florida, we know that the homeowners who prepare before June 1st sleep a lot easier during storm season. Here is exactly what we tell every customer.

item.title: Single Hung vs Double Hung Impact Windows: Which Is Right for Your Home?
item.description: We install both single hung and double hung impact windows every week, and homeowners always ask which one is better. Here is the honest comparison we share during every consultation.

item.description: One of the first things we tell homeowners is how long the project will take. After 15 years, we have our timeline down to a science. Here is what to expect.

item.title: Impact Windows vs Regular Windows: What South Florida Homeowners Need to Know
item.description: One of the most common questions we get from homeowners is what makes impact windows different from regular windows. After thousands of installations, here is our straightforward answer.

item.title: Building Permits for Impact Windows in South Florida: A Complete Guide
item.description: We pull permits for every installation we do, no exceptions. Here is how the permit process works in Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties.

item.title: Why South Florida Homeowners Need Impact Windows Before Hurricane Season

item.title: How to Get the Best Deal on Impact Windows in South Florida
item.description: We would rather give you honest advice on how to get the best value than see you overpay with another contractor. Here are the insider tips we share with every homeowner.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: How to Clean and Maintain Your Impact Windows in South Florida
item.description: We always give our customers a maintenance rundown after installation. Here are the same cleaning and care tips we share to keep your impact windows performing perfectly.

item.description: We understand that impact windows are a significant investment. That is why we help our customers explore every financing option available to make it work within their budget.
item.author: info@floridaimpactwindowsdoors.com (Roy Bach, Owner)

item.title: Impact Windows vs Hurricane Shutters: Which Is Better for South Florida?
item.description: We get asked about shutters versus impact windows on almost every consultation. Here is our honest take as contractors who have installed both and seen them tested by real storms.

item.description: We tell every customer that impact windows are not just an expense, they are an investment that pays you back. Here is how we have seen the numbers work out over and over.

item.title: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows: What Homeowners Need to Know
item.description: We deal with Florida Building Code on every single project. Here is what every homeowner needs to understand about code requirements before starting a window replacement.

item.title: How Much Do Impact Windows Cost in South Florida? A 2025 Price Guide
item.description: We believe in being upfront about pricing. After 15 years in the business, here is our honest breakdown of what impact windows really cost in South Florida.

item.title: Modern Window Design Trends for South Florida Homes in 2025
item.description: We are seeing some incredible design trends in impact windows right now. From floor-to-ceiling glass to matte black frames, here is what our customers are choosing in 2025.
